Japan.cuisineSummary: Japanese cuisine balances seasonality, craftsmanship, and respect for ingredients with playful everyday food culture. From ramen and rice bowls to matcha and beautifully packaged snacks, it rewards curiosity with precision, comfort, and umami.
Japan.snackCulture: Seasonal limited editions, regional specialties, convenience-store creativity, and sweet-savory flavor experimentation.
Japan.diningTraditions: Seasonal appreciation, careful presentation, shared dishes with individual portions, and hospitality rituals.

South Korea.cuisineSummary: Korean cuisine is bold, communal, and full of contrast, where fermented depth, spicy heat, grilled comfort, and refreshing side dishes often meet at one table.
South Korea.snackCulture: Convenience-store snacks, seaweed crisps, spicy noodles, sweet drinks, and limited-edition treats drive discovery.
South Korea.diningTraditions: Shared plates, table grilling, banchan side dishes, communal meals, and celebratory drinking rituals.

India.cuisineSummary: Indian cuisine is vast, vibrant, and deeply regional, built on spices, grains, legumes, breads, curries, grilling, sweets, and centuries of culinary tradition.
India.snackCulture: Chaat, samosas, sweets, fried snacks, savory mixes, and regional packaged snacks make exploration easy.
India.diningTraditions: Family feasts, festival foods, thali dining, street-food culture, and region-specific hospitality.

Morocco.cuisineSummary: Moroccan cuisine is fragrant, colorful, and layered, combining slow cooking, warm spices, preserved lemons, olives, couscous, mint tea, and generous hospitality.
Morocco.snackCulture: Pastries, nuts, date sweets, fried bites, and sweet-savory snacks are common discovery points.
Morocco.diningTraditions: Shared platters, Friday couscous traditions, hospitality feasts, and festive family dining.
